opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin is suddenly turning completely white around my mouth and eyes. It looks like my pigment is being eaten away, and I'm terrified it's spreading everywhere.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden spreading white patches on my face — I'm panicking that it's a serious disease.

symptom_narrative.onset: small white patches on the hands months ago; rapid facial spread over 2 weeks after stress
symptom_narrative.character: completely chalk-white, smooth patches with sharp edges; no scaling/itch/pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: very distressed/panicking; the patches themselves don't hurt or itch
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: around the mouth and eyes, and the backs of the hands; symmetrical
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a patch of white hairs in one eyebrow; no scaling, itch or pain; feels normal to touch

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: period of intense stress before the facial spread

objective_data_offered.value: several completely chalk-white smooth patches with sharp edges around the mouth/eyes and on the backs of the hands, symmetrical; a patch of white hairs in one eyebrow; the skin feels normal (no scaling/lumps)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; cannot test sensation or Wood's lamp

objective_data_offered.value: feels well otherwise; normal sensation in the patches as far as I can tell
